core_payload.content:
# Key Agents User Lane

## 1. Boundary
Key Agents User Lane owns the user-facing intelligence roles that let one human speak to the system, navigate sovereign memory, and receive graph-grounded help without dropping directly into deeper orchestration mechanics. This sub-hub exists so the user lane stays distinct from mining, planning, governance, stewardship, and background swarm work.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `personal-ai-assistant`, `chat-to-capsules`, and `capsule-librarian-agent`. Together they define the front-door partner surface, the graph-grounded conversational ingress path, and the vault-native retrieval role that keep user interaction legible and evidence-bound.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own extraction work, planning compression, maintenance workers, validation authority, or background swarm behavior. Those belong to `mining-lane`, `planning-lane`, `stewardship-lane`, `governance-lane`, and `swarm-lane`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Key Agents User Lane groups the user-facing intelligence surfaces of N1Hub: the assistant carrier, graph-grounded conversational ingress, and vault-native retrieval role. It keeps human interaction separate from mining, governance, and background swarm mechanics. This matters because one lane should own the boundary where a person speaks to the system, navigates memory, and receives graph-grounded help without collapsing that experience into deeper orchestration, maintenance, autonomous runtime concerns, or governance review loops.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of key-agents hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 28 outbound to ≤9.
